programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Adapted Active8
programs.description: With a variety of activities to enjoy, Active8 will keep you moving. Join us for co-operative games, arts, sports, basic cooking, swimming, physical activity workshops, community walks and more.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Sports
programs.description: Calling all sports fans! In this camp you will have the opportunity to learn the fundamentals of play in a variety of sports. Through drills, practice and games, enhance your skills and have some fun.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your sport skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Sports
programs.description: Calling all sports fans! In this camp you will have the opportunity to learn the fundamentals of play in a variety of sports. Through drills, practice and games, enhance your skills and have some fun.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your sport skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

programs.name: Gymnastics - Advanced
programs.description: Flexibility, strength, coordination and balance, gymnastics has it all. With a focus on strength training and technique, you will work on front handsprings, safe dismounts and how to connect movements to create tumbling passes on various apparatuses. Prerequisite: Gymnastics (Intermediate).

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Adapted Ultra Swim - Beginner
programs.description: Swim at your own pace. Discover water movements through sensory cues such as look, listen and feel and practice fun ways to enter and exit the pool to gain comfort in the water. Learn essential swimming skills such as floating, kicking, and gliding and understand the importance of water safety and how to be safe in and around water environments. Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or aiscw@toronto.ca. Please Note: External support persons attending with you shall enter water as required and can attend at no charge.

programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Adapted Ultra Swim - Beginner
programs.description: Swim at your own pace. Discover water movements through sensory cues such as look, listen and feel and practice fun ways to enter and exit the pool to gain comfort in the water. Learn essential swimming skills such as floating, kicking, and gliding and understand the importance of water safety and how to be safe in and around water environments. Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or aiscw@toronto.ca. Please Note: External support persons attending with you shall enter water as required and can attend at no charge.

Learn essential swimming skills such as floating, kicking, and gliding and understand the importance of water safety and how to be safe in and around water environments. Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or aiscw@toronto.ca. Please Note: External support persons attending with you shall enter water as required and can attend at no charge.

programs.name: Gymnastics - Intermediate
programs.description: Flexibility, strength, coordination and balance, gymnastics has it all. Prerequisite: Gymnastics (Beginner).

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.description: Flexibility, strength, coordination and balance, gymnastics has it all. With a focus on fun and safety, you will learn and practice basic skills such as walks, jumps, rolls and turns on various apparatuses. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Hockey Skills
programs.description: Come and enjoy Canada’s national pastime—hockey! This activity introduces new hockey players to basic hockey skills and the rules of the game to prepare you for a house league or recreational play. Learn puck and stick handling, passing and shooting, as well as game strategy through a combination of lessons, drills and play.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1, Learn to Skate - Beginner or able to skate one length of the ice surface.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage, a hockey stick, hockey skates, hockey gloves and a neck guard.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Sports
programs.description: Calling all sports fans! In this camp you will have the opportunity to learn the fundamentals of play in a variety of sports. Through drills, practice and games, enhance your skills and have some fun.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your sport skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

programs.name: Intermediate First Aid with CPR-C & AED
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society (in accordance with CSA Z1210:24). Learn comprehensive first aid training covering spinal injuries, heat/cold injuries, bone and joint injuries, burns, medical emergencies, and the proper use of automated external defibrillators.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered with the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

Practical skills are assessed through scenarios and knowledge will be tested through a written evaluation.Prerequisite: Current Intermediate First Aid (CSA Z1210:24).For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered with the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Introduction to Machine Knitting: Bags and Home Decor
programs.description: Using our LK150 knitting machines and your own yarn, create your custom designs like bags, pillowcases and more! Students will learnthe fundamentals of machine knitting including skills like casting on and off, forming slip knots and stitches, and checking tension. Thisworkshop requires an additional $15 material fee (cash only), payable to instructor for practice materials. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Introduction to Oil Painting
programs.description: Learn to work with oil paints, with this step-by step introduction to guiding you through a range of foundational techniques and Approaches. Participants will learn how to plan and complete an oil painting while exploring key concepts such as underpainting, fat over-thin, glazing, impasto and alla prima. Designed for beginners, the course provides a solid foundation in both the materials and methods of oil painting in a supportive, hands-on environment. Please note that only non-toxic, solvent-free mediums and thinners are permitted.

programs.name: Introduction to Punch Needle Rug Hooking
programs.description: Discover the art of punch  needle rug hooking in this fun, hands-on one-day workshop! Learn the basics of  the technique, including how to use the punch needle tool, set up your fabric,  and create textured loops. You’ll design and complete a small, personalized piece.  This workshop includes a $30 material fee payable to the instructor.

programs.name: Introduction to Weaving
programs.description: Whether you're a complete beginner or looking  to refine your skills, this course is designed to guide you through the  fundamentals and creative possibilities of weaving. You'll learn the essentials  of weaving techniques, including loom setup, winding the warp, basic weave  structures, finishing techniques, terminology, loom descriptions and how to  read weaving patterns. Participants are welcome to bring their own yarns and  projects ideas. For  those who are new to weaving, beginner projects will be provided and yarn can  be purchased from the instructor, for a fee between $20-30.

programs.name: Bronze Cross
programs.description: Bronze Cross begins the transition from lifesaving to lifeguarding. Candidates strengthen and expand their lifesaving skills and begin to apply the principles and techniques of active surveillance in aquatic facilities. Bronze Cross emphasizes the importance of teamwork and communication in preventing and responding to aquatic emergencies. Bronze Cross is a prerequisite for advanced training in the Society’s National Lifeguard and leadership certification programs. Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres in 11 minutes or less by the course exam date. This course is generally offered in an extended nine-class format. Prerequisite: Bronze Medallion and Emergency First Aid with CPR-B (current or expired) or Standard First Aid with CPR-C  from an approved provider.Equipment: Bring your copy of the Canadian Lifesaving manual received in Bronze Medallion.

programs.name: Bronze Cross & Intermediate First Aid with CPR-C & AED
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. The Bronze Cross certification begins the transition from lifesaving to lifeguarding. Candidates strengthen and expand their lifesaving skills and begin to apply the principles and techniques of active surveillance in aquatic facilities. Bronze Cross emphasizes the importance of teamwork and communication in preventing and responding to aquatic emergencies. Bronze Cross is a prerequisite for advanced training in the Society’s National Lifeguard and leadership certification programs. Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres in 11 minutes or less by the course exam date. The Intermediate First Aid CPR-C & AED certification (in accordance with CSA Z1210:24) provides comprehensive first-aid training, covering spinal injuries, heat/cold injuries, bone and joint injuries, burns, medical emergencies, and the use of automated external defibrillators. You must bring the Canadian Lifesaving manual received in Bronze Medallion. This course is offered in an intensive weekend format.Prerequisite: Bronze Medallion and one of the following first aid certifications (current or expired) from an approved provider: Emergency First Aid with CPR-B or Basic First Aid with CPR-C & AED or Standard First Aid with CPR-C or Intermediate First Aid with CPR-C & AED.Equipment: Bring your copy of the Canadian Lifesaving manual received in Bronze Medallion and current Canadian First Aid ManualFor information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Japanese Paper Marbling
programs.description: Create beautiful, marbled patterns on different  types of paper in this exciting workshop. The patterns are the result of color  floated on a liquid, and then carefully transferred to paper. Students will be  able to take home and use their unique creations. A materials fee in cash of  $10.00 will be due to the course instructor at the start of the class.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Japanese Paper Marbling
programs.description: Create beautiful, marbled patterns on different  types of paper in this exciting workshop. The patterns are the result of color  floated on a liquid, and then carefully transferred to paper. Students will be  able to take home and use their unique creations. A materials fee in cash of  $10.00 will be due to the course instructor at the start of the class.

programs.name: Junior Leader Training
programs.description: This course is designed for youth aged 14 to 17 who are interested in volunteering as a Junior Leader at the Expressions Summer Art Camp at Cedar Ridge. Leadership and basic arts skills will be developed during the program. Participants will receive a certificate confirming completion of the course. Limited volunteer positions available on a first come first served basis. This course is mandatory for all volunteers at Expressions. The minimum age requirement of 14 years is firm.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Sports
programs.description: Calling all sports fans! In this camp you will have the opportunity to learn the fundamentals of play in a variety of sports. Through drills, practice and games, enhance your skills and have some fun.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your sport skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

In addition to focusing on developing and fine-tuning your swim skills, you will also enjoy games, arts and sports. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.
